SUCCESS_MASKING CLEARED pass: 28 judge probes confirm 0.65.0-rc1, 0.64.20-beta, 0.64.20-rc1, -dirty, SNAPSHOT+build, 2026.07.01, 0.65, 0.64.2 all WARN + receipt; garbage/empty/rc!=0 stay silent with an unreadable receipt; reverting the filter turns the pre-release stderr assertions RED

residual: `cmux v0.65.0` still silent on stderr (receipt written). Remedy is normalization (strip leading v), NOT widening the shape test - accepting v would make v0.64.20 compare unequal to the pin and cry wolf on the same release

two of six new assertions do not discriminate: grep -qF "$v" "$VER_MARKER" passes against the UNREADABLE receipt too, since that receipt embeds the raw line; proved by mutation (clean-filter revert still passes both). Assert receipt KIND, not substring

mutation row 3 does not reproduce at the claimed 73/4: filter-only revert = 75/2, full round-1 parser revert = 71/6. RED and discriminating either way, but 73/4 needs a TWO-part mutation, violating the log's own one-anchor rule

NEW noise path: 2>/dev/null does not suppress a redirection failure, so an unwritable PANE_STATE_DIR makes the documented-silent unreadable path emit 'Permission denied' once per dispatch (verified). New to this commit; fix is { printf ... > "$marker"; } 2>/dev/null

receipt still write-only: nothing in panes/hooks/statusline reads it (re-grepped). Self-clearing + the new cmux-layout.sh back-pointer are real upgrades, but forensics is not notification; the UNREADABLE receipt is invisible by construction. Cheapest reader: statusline

both rounds' verdict artifacts uncommitted (verdicts.jsonl modified, round-1 .md untracked) to keep HEAD stable for judge-guard; a git clean -fd right now destroys two rounds of audit trail. Commit immediately after gh pr create

TRACEABILITY RESTORED concern->pass: rewritten branch log spot-checked against code on ~20 claims, all correct; judge independently re-ran ALL SIX falsification rows and every number reproduces EXACTLY (73/6, 65/14, 75/4, 78/1, 77/2, 78/1) including the 75/4 that replaces round-2's non-reproducing 73/4; boundary probe reproduces the documented table; suite 170->195 and cmux-exec 54->79 measured against a scratch checkout of main, not quoted

SUCCESS_MASKING REGRESSED pass->concern (NEW, judge-found): the brace fix covers BOTH receipt writes but the new regression test at cmux-exec.test.sh:446-456 drives only the MISMATCH branch (version 0.65.0). Unbracing the mismatch write IS caught (78/1). Shipped code correct on both paths; only the net has a hole, over the exact line the commit exists to fix. One-line remedy: loop the RO case over 0.65.0 AND garbage

receipt still write-only: grep confirms 1 writer (cmux.sh:46), 1 test reader, 2 doc pointers, 0 code readers. Condition: the first reader must surface the UNREADABLE kind too, which is invisible by construction

v0.65.0 still silent on stderr (receipt written); unchanged and endorsed twice - remedy is normalization, not a wider shape test

commit message says 'Suite 186 -> 195'; prior HEAD 758b1fa measured 193 (judge ran it). Branch log's cumulative 170->195 is correct; only the trailer skips a hop, reading as +9 tests when the commit added 2

branch log line 3 credits 'the round-2 observability judge' without saying which branch's - that is the PARENT branch (pane-layout-v2) round 2, while this branch now has its own round 2; line 66 disambiguates, line 3 does not

three rounds of judge artifacts still uncommitted (verdicts.jsonl modified + 3 untracked verdict .md files); deliberate for judge-guard HEAD stability but one git clean -fd destroys the whole review record - commit right after gh pr create
a mismatch nags every dispatch until a human edits the pin; endorsed as the correct trade, but 0.64.20-rc1 warns permanently against a 0.64.20 pin

Mitigation is the 4th advisory lesson for the same failure mode; the prior 3 failed. gh pr create --draft closes the window enforceably and passes judge-guard unchanged, but is considered nowhere in the repo.
The structural framing covers all 3 stranded commits when only dbe9289 is forced; 9107345 was a deliberate post-creation push exploiting judge-guard being create-only.
Escalation threshold internally inconsistent: heading says 3rd occurrence, remedy waits for a 4th.
Guaranteed merge conflict with feature/judge-terminal-enforcement on CODING_MEMORY.md (198 vs 386) and both verdicts.jsonl; undocumented on either branch.
CODING_MEMORY.md 386 lines vs its own 200-line ceiling, +4 on this branch; third consecutive branch to move it the wrong way.
No ADR under docs/decisions/ for a three-time structural workflow failure.
This verdict itself hits the same window: it cannot be committed before gh pr create without invalidating itself.

Governance-model flip (include->exclude) of a shipped, hook-enforced system reverses the deliberate 'plan implementers are skill-routed' stance; user-confirmed and degrades never-blocks, but a real adjacent-behavior change and the always-on gate stub carries a now-stale claim that must be corrected not appended

No ADR named in the doc-touch list for the governance flip; repo convention (ADR 0007 precedent: 'pane-orchestration supersedes judge-terminal-enforcement') says a direction-pivoting change to a shipped system earns one

Standing fake-binary masking risk: new open_tab + live-pane-count logic will again be fake-binary-tested; ADR 0008 documents this exact trap biting live cmux twice while all tests stayed green. Design's mitigation (probe real cmux tab primitive first, record as fixture) is correct and must be a hard gate before the adapter is trusted

Liveness predicate for 'concurrent panes' is a new mechanism - the dispatcher has no explicit completion-marker read today; inferred from state/runs/. Least-proven piece, correctly flagged as assumption 1 with an overflow-to-inline degrade

Design claims verified against real guard/dispatcher/adapters/conf/ADR 0008 - all match; confidence high. Unprovable-at-design-stage items (cmux tab primitive, liveness feasibility) correctly deferred to an implementation-time probe

Include->exclude governance flip reverses the deliberate 'plan implementers are skill-routed' stance; user-confirmed and degrades-never-blocks, but a real adjacent-behavior change and the always-on gate stub's now-stale claim must be corrected in place, not appended
No ADR named in the doc-touch list for the governance flip; repo convention (ADR 0007 precedent) says a direction-pivoting change to a shipped system earns one

Liveness predicate for 'concurrent panes' is a new mechanism inferred from state/runs/ with no completion-marker read today; least-proven piece, correctly flagged as assumption 1 with an overflow-to-inline degrade

Round-2 change is a verbatim gherkin reformat of the acceptance scenarios only; design trajectory unchanged from round 1; unprovable-at-design-stage items correctly deferred to an implementation-time probe

Plan implementers still move skill-routed -> policy-governed on a shipped system; user-confirmed and degrades-never-blocks, but a real routing change, and the always-on gate stub's 'plan implementers are skill-routed' clause is now stale and must be corrected in place (its 'judges are hook-enforced' clause stays true under the three-lane model)

Still no ADR named in the doc-touch list, now for an explicit governance-model change ('not a clean include->exclude flip'); repo convention (ADR 0007 precedent) says a direction-pivoting change to a shipped hook-enforced system earns one

Assumption 1 now does double duty - derive live worker count from state/runs/ AND tag each run's lane so judge runs are excluded; both feed the pane-vs-tab number. Correctly flagged with a conservative-count / overflow-inline degrade; least-proven piece

Judge-not-counted acceptance scenario must be tested on real run-dir fixtures, not faked liveness, or a green test masks the state derivation (ADR 0008 pattern); separate open_tab fake-binary risk correctly mitigated by the mandated live cmux probe fixture

The three-lane revision is a net correctness improvement over the two-lane framing: splitting the judges into their own always-paned lane preserves the judge-guard governance invariant that inline would otherwise have silenced
